channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: AI giant Anthropic files for US IPO as investors bet big on AI future
item.description: Anthropic, which operates AI chatbot Claude, did not disclose the size or the terms of the offering.

item.title: Israeli strike damages hospital in southern Lebanon
item.description: An Israeli attack near Jabal Amel Hospital in Tyre has destroyed wards and medical equipment.

item.title: Ethiopians head to polls as millions excluded from elections
item.description: Ethiopians head to polls as millions excluded from elections.

item.title: Activists plaster missing posters around Mexico stadiums ahead of World Cup
item.description: Activists put up missing persons posters to raise awareness of the disappearance crisis ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Former general for Syria’s Assad pleads not guilty in torture trial
item.description: Brigadier General Khaled al-Halabi faces charges of aggravated torture, coercion, sexual coercion and bodily harm.

item.title: Nvidia unveils new chip to bring AI directly to personal computers
item.description: Nvidia's new chip will power new Windows laptop and desktop computer models to make 'AI personal computers'.

item.title: Can cohosts Canada reach the knockout stages at World Cup 2026?
item.description: Canada are playing only their third World Cup, but have high hopes for a talented squad under coach Jesse Marsch.

item.title: International court rejects Rwanda’s claim over UK migration deal
item.description: Rwanda's $134m claim has been thrown out, which bodes ill for other governments seeking 'return hubs'.

item.title: Protest outside Kenya’s planned US Ebola quarantine centre
item.description: Hundreds rally against plan to send US nationals exposed to the virus to Laikipia Air Base.

item.title: Lebanon’s social grocery store fighting rising costs and displacement
item.description: ‘Man wa Salwa’ is a community-run social grocery store helping hundreds of families access affordable essentials.

item.title: Who will replace Newsom? What to know about California’s primary elections
item.description: The most populous US state has attracted attention for its contentious gubernatorial race with 61 candidates.

item.title: Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon and Gaza threaten US ceasefire talks
item.description: Iranian officials demand 'ceasefire on all fronts' as Israel deepens Lebanon invasion and threatens to bomb Beirut.

item.title: As Israel pushes past the Litani, Lebanese question the purpose of UNIFIL
item.description: Israel has pushed past the Litani River, with the UNFIL peacekeeping force helpless to prevent an invasion.

item.title: Indian PM Modi meets Myanmar military gov’t leader in New Delhi
item.description: Myanmar opposition groups criticise India for hosting Min Aung Hlaing, but India says engagement is best way forward.

item.title: Lebanon latest: Israel captures more land in the south
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr brings you the latest from Lebanon as Israel expands its occupation there.

item.title: Egypt’s new monorail offers a modern ride, but Cairo is still not convinced
item.description: Cairo's East Nile monorail opened to passengers this month as a flagship of Egypt's infrastructure ambitions.

item.title: Sacked Arsenal staffer doesn’t regret speaking out for Palestine
item.description: Mark Bonnick is suing Arsenal football club for unfair dismissal, saying he was sacked for supporting Palestine.

item.title: Huge explosion at Malta fireworks factory
item.description: A large explosion has torn through a fireworks factory in northern Malta, damaging buildings several kilometres away.

item.title: ‘Spoiled insulin’: Sudan war disrupts drug supplies, fuelling smuggling
item.description: As the conflict destroys local production, Sudanese patients are forced to rely on expensive, smuggled medicines.

item.title: Hungary’s Magyar to amend the constitution to remove President Tamas Sulyok
item.description: After coming to power in April, PM Peter Magyar had given Sulyok a deadline of Sunday to leave office.

item.title: How the world failed Ryan and Yaman
item.description: An Israeli strike killed my children. What pains me is not just their loss but also the normalisation of their murder.

item.title: Ukraine’s forcibly transferred children must not be a bargaining chip
item.description: Russia must face more pressure to fulfil its legal obligations to return the Ukrainian children.

item.title: Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikes
item.description: Videos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area.

item.title: How could El Nino reshape tropical storms around the world this year?
item.description: El Nino tends to reduce hurricanes in the Atlantic while increasing storms in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Greece reopens Syrian and Afghan asylum cases, hoping for returns
item.description: Greece's migration minister recently said Athens does not share common values with 'hardcore Islam'.
